game_name: M.U.S.H.A.: Metallic Uniframe Super Hybrid Armor

design_philosophy.core_loop: Collect power chips to upgrade main gun, switch between three special weapons (laser/fire/shield), manage two equipped drones with configurable attack modes, navigate vertically scrolling stages while destroying enemy formations
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management via weapon drops and power-up loss on hit; risk/reward of equipping special weapons that grant survival protection but can be stripped away
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot an advanced mecha (MUSHA) with customizable weapon loadouts to defend Earth from a rogue supercomputer threat in 2290
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate aesthetic pivot from previous Aleste entries toward Japanese visual identity (Noh masks, feudal-inspired design) paired with speed metal soundtrack; rejected mechanical synth sounds in favor of humanized instrumental tones; technical showcase of Genesis parallax scrolling with Doppler effect sound modulation

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system; losing a special weapon preserves life, but getting hit without equipped weapon costs a life
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players configure drone attack modes (forward, behind, rotating) across six positioning options to adapt tactical approach; weapon choice affects survivability and offensive patterns

progression.upgrade_system: Power Chips incrementally upgrade main gun damage; same special weapon pickups stack upgrades; every three Power Chips grants a stored drone (max two equipped, overflow stored)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Weapon types and drone configurations unlocked through stage progression and enemy drops
replayability_hooks: Drone mode optimization across six attack configurations
Special weapon selection strategy (laser vs fire vs shield trade-offs)
Pattern memorization for stage navigation and enemy formations

world_building_techniques: Character design aesthetic (Noh mask boss imagery reflects Japanese feudal fusion with sci-fi mecha)
Setting premise: year 2290, rogue supercomputer Dire 51 attacking from space
Protagonist Terri as pilot of cutting-edge military technology

boss_design.philosophy: Visually distinctive encounters leveraging Japanese aesthetic; at least one boss features Noh mask design; difficulty increased in retrospective reception suggesting pattern-heavy encounters

faction_indicators: Human military (player pilots MUSHA for Earth defense)
danger_signals: Space-based supercomputer attack from orbital position
environmental_storytelling: Valley stage parallax scrolling suggests environmental depth and technical sophistication
Falling tiles and dynamic enemy depth positioning imply environmental hazards beyond direct combat
Japanese aesthetic in stage design and boss imagery creates cultural coherence

sources_used: Wikipedia - MUSHA article (gameplay mechanics, development philosophy, aesthetic direction, composer intent, reception context)
